The breakthrough idea is mechanistically sound and directly addresses the shape-loss on delayed inhibitor curves. Let me quickly verify the Knowles secondary-nucleation structure before finalizing, then implement.

The key physical principle from Knowles/Cohen master-equation kinetics: fibril mass growth has distinct microscopic channels — primary nucleation (from free monomer), elongation (linear in fibril mass, monomer-fed), and surface-catalyzed secondary nucleation (superlinear/quadratic in fibril mass). Inhibitors typically act on specific microscopic steps. Applying inhibition only to autocatalytic channels (elongation + secondary) preserves cd=0 clean curves while delaying takeoff at high cd — this is exactly what shape-loss timing at 10/25/50% rewards.
